metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_4159
metadata.comment:
Rationale: By using Nrg3 -/- mice slices of the hippocampus, the authors find that the formation of excitatory synapses is impaired by the loss of Nrg3 by measuring GluA4 puncta (Figure 1H). This is also observed in primary cultures obtained from Nrg3 -/- mice (Figure 2D)

Nrg3 is provided presynaptically, as co-culturing primary neurons from Nrg3-/- mice with wild type primary neurons demonstrates that Nrg3 is observed in spines of all neurons, both wild type and Nrg3 -/-, indicating that Nrg3 is not provided postsynaptically but it is produced presynaptically by wild type neurons and provided in trans to Nrg3 -/- neurons (Figure 2F, F', F').. Experimental description: "The following mutant mouse strains were used in this study: Nrg3, Nrg3 <tm1.1Cbm>...", "Cortex, hippocampus, and striatum were dissected from juvenile mice (P30-60) and..."
